text: To wake up
To get up
To run into
To look after
To give up
To turn down
To fill out
To drop off
To pick up
To sort out
To put off
To get along
I filled out the form.
I picked her up at six.
I ran into a friend.
Please look after the kids.
Tom, my mornings are so busy right now. I wake up at six and I barely stop until night.
I know that feeling! Do you get up right away, or do you stay in bed for a while?
I stay in bed for ten minutes. Then I get up, make coffee, and drop off my daughter at school.
That is a lot before work! Do you ever run into people you know on the way?
All the time! This morning I ran into my neighbor, and we get along so well that we talked for ages.
Nice. And how is the paperwork going? You had a lot of forms to fill out, right?
So many forms. I almost gave up on one of them, but I never give up for long.
Good for you. Do you have plans tonight, or will you turn down everything and rest?
I have to pick up my daughter and look after her. I do not want to put off my rest, so no plans tonight!
I wake up early, but I do not get up right away.
I drop off my daughter and pick her up after work.
I had to fill out a lot of forms this week.
I ran into an old friend and we get along well.
I do not like to put off my rest.

Most people think my mornings are calm, but the truth is they are a little crazy. I wake up at half past five, and honestly, I do not get up right away; I lie in bed and check the time twice. When I finally get up, I make breakfast and drop off my son at school on the way to the office. At work, the first thing I do is sort out my emails and fill out the reports from the day before. Some days I want to give up before nine o'clock, but I never do. The best part of my morning is small: I almost always run into the same friendly barista at the coffee shop, and that little chat sets up my whole day. A good routine is not about doing everything perfectly; it is about not putting off the things that matter.

Hi Ana Paula, it is Sarah! I am calling because my week has been completely full, and I had to tell someone. On Monday, I had to fill out a huge stack of forms for my new apartment, and I nearly gave up halfway through. Then, on Tuesday, I ran into our old classmate Lucas at the supermarket, of all places. We get along so well that we talked for almost an hour! The rest of the week, I have been looking after my little niece while my sister is away, so I pick her up from daycare every afternoon. I actually turned down a dinner invitation last night because I was too tired. Anyway, I do not want to put off calling you any longer. Tell me about your week, and let us sort out a day to meet. Talk soon, bye!

On a busy morning, I wake up before my alarm. I get up, make coffee, and drop off my daughter at school. At work, I fill out forms and sort out small problems. On my way home, I run into a friend at the store. In the evening, I pick up my daughter and look after her.

I had to fill out three forms.
I ran into an old friend yesterday.
I look after my daughter in the evening.
I never give up when things get hard.
I wake up early, but I do not get up right away.
I ran into an old friend at the pharmacy.
I pick up my daughter and look after her.
I get along really well with my neighbors.
I drop off my daughter and pick her up after work.
